action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 14 ran local eval with ImprovedPlanner and scored 0.9018 on 15 training maps (100% success), but the evaluation was never submitted to the full eval set. The current best eval score remains 0.6118 from iteration 7. The predict.py visible in context is truncated, so I need to ensure the file is complete on disk with a robust main() loop. Analysis: The discrepancy between local (0.90) and previous eval scores (0.60) for greedy planners suggests the 15-map sample is easier, but ImprovedPlanner (with target commitment, frontier fallback, anti-backtracking, and step-budget guard) might still improve generalization. The priority is to measure its true eval performance. I will rewrite predict.py completely to eliminate any risk of truncation/corruption, include bulletproof exception handling in the eval loop, regenerate the dummy checkpoint, run a quick smoke test, and immediately submit evaluation.
